task_completion.description: Open the target document in Microsoft Word
task_completion.visual_evidence: Microsoft Word 2010 is open and the title bar displays 'Regulatory_Submission_Draft.docx'.

task_completion.description: Open the Restrict Formatting and Editing pane
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Restrict Formatting and Editing' pane is visible on the right side of the Word window.

task_completion.description: Set editing restrictions to Comments only
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Restrict Editing pane under 'Editing restrictions', the checkbox 'Allow only this type of editing in the document:' is checked, and 'Comments' is selected in the dropdown menu.

task_completion.description: Apply an editing exception for Everyone to the REVIEWER NOTES section
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text 'REVIEWER NOTES' is highlighted/selected in the main document, and the 'Everyone' checkbox is checked under the 'Exceptions' section in the Restrict Editing pane.

task_completion.description: Enforce the protection with a password
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Start Enforcing Protection' dialog is visible with asterisks in the password fields, or the Restrict Editing pane shows that protection is active (e.g., displaying a 'Stop Protection' button).

task_completion.description: Save the changes to the document
task_completion.visual_evidence: The agent clicks the Save icon (floppy disk) in the Quick Access Toolbar, or uses the File > Save menu option.

integrity.description: The agent used Microsoft Word to perform the task
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshots show Microsoft Word 2010 being used, not a different text editor or word processor.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the Word GUI to set protections
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent is seen clicking through the Word ribbon and Restrict Editing pane, rather than running a script or terminal command to modify the file.

integrity.description: No terminal or scripting tools were used to bypass the GUI
integrity.visual_evidence: There are no PowerShell, Command Prompt, or Python windows open executing commands to change document permissions.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target file: 'Regulatory_Submission_Draft.docx'. Protection type: 'Comments'. Exception group: 'Everyone'. Target section text: 'REVIEWER NOTES'. Password: 'Review2024'.
